name: Random Payload Packet
description: Typical packet with arbitrary 50-byte payload containing random data

payload_structure.bytes: Random pattern: FA 3C 7E 9B 2D 41 8F B0 C3 55 ... (50 bytes total)
payload_structure.purpose: Demonstrate CRC calculation with irregular data patterns

length_field_verification: Length field must equal 0x0032 (50 in decimal, little-endian: 32 00)

crc_details.note: CRC computed from actual firmware execution on this exact payload

validation_rules: Total packet size must equal 60 bytes
Payload must be exactly 50 bytes in exact order
CRC16-Modbus over first 58 bytes must match
Byte order must be little-endian throughout
test_purposes: Verify CRC with irregular/random data pattern
Verify no data corruption through transport
edge_cases: Payload is neither power-of-2 nor round number (50)
CRC influenced by all payload bytes
real_world_scenario: Configuration bulk write where 50 bytes of settings are transmitted to firmware. Demonstrates that arbitrary binary patterns are preserved exactly through protocol.
notes:
This vector demonstrates:
1. Mid-size payload (50 bytes - typical for complex commands)
2. Arbitrary binary data (not text or pattern)
3. CRC calculation with varied byte values
4. Payload integrity assurance
5. Protocol handles any data without corruption
verification_method: Generated from firmware reference implementation with actual payload data
interoperability_note: Any implementation passing this vector can handle arbitrary payload data correctly
